title: Agarose pads for microscopy
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"e3uke","text":"This protocols describe the steps required for the preparation of agarose pads for live cell fluorescent microscopy. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"at67e","text":"All steps described in this protocol are intended to be conducted in a research laboratory.  Follow aseptic procedures.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}

materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"qjfPT","text":"MATERIALS","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":null,"entityRanges":null,"data":null},{"key":"HwEiP","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":null,"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":null}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"reagents","mutability":"","data":{"id":580547,"guid":"42600488341F11EE9D4302C0B41BC903","name":" Frame-Seal™ in situ PCR and Hybridization Slide Chambers 15 x 15 mm 65 µl","mol_weight":0,"sku":"SLF0601","url":"https://www.bio-rad.com/en-ca/sku/slf0601-frame-seal-situ-pcr-hybridization-slide-chambers-15-x-15-mm-65-ul?ID=slf0601","cas_number":"","rrid":"","vendor":{"id":1,"name":"Contributed by users","link":"https://www.protocols.io","is_requested":false,"image":{"source":"/img/vendors/1.png","placeholder":"/img/vendors/1.png"}},"is_public":true,"can_edit":false}}}}
steps: Check all the necessary material: Glass slide Cover slip Agarose (Agar should work as well, but probably with less imaging quality) Media or buffer Chamber or well forming stick (ex: Frame-Seal™ in situ PCR and Hybridization Slide Chambers, 15 x 15 mm, 65 µl) Tube or flask for melting agarose 1 mL plastic tips

Melt the agarose in the desired concentration ( 1.5 Mass / % volume were tested ) using a microwave, with interval mixing. Using the desired liquid. (e.g. Media for keeping the cells alive ) Keep the melted agarose in a plate heater at 70 °C .

Cut a 1mL tips to increase the point diameter for easier liquid handling of the agarose. Separate a glass slide to use as cover during pad preparation Stick the chamber forming material to a glass slide, maintaining the plastic barrier in the exposed side (Alternativaly you can follow the instructions at dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.kfrctm6 for a option without the chamber sticks) Add a excess of melted agarose to the well, and cover it with another glass slide for shaping the agarose pad Remove the glass slide, by sliding it carefully to the side Cutted plastic tip Melted agarose added Solidifying agarose Glass slide removed after solidification

Add the desired amount of sample to the agarose pad. ( 5-6 1 µL drops were tested | Be aware of cell concentration if the goal is to see individual cells ) Wait the drops to dry 00:05:00 to 00:10:00 ( Specially important for cells with motility ) Carefully remove the plastic barrier exposing the adhesive face of the well forming material Add a cover slip, sealing the well. ( Avoids dehydration of the agarose pad, important for long microscopy experiments) Samples added to agarose pad Glass slide ready for microscopy
creator: Joao Vitor Molino
